page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 180 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 1440.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware. The processor features a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration via AES, VAES, and SHA_ni.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 277249.17 and a Passmark CPU score of 124350.65. Memory performance is highly competitive, registering a top-tier read-only small block cached bandwidth of 13485.99 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 177.32 GB/sec. The server demonstrates strong single-core performance with a Geekbench single-core score of 2009.0. In database operations, the instance achieved 210.86 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. Large language model inference benchmarks show strong to top-tier performance, processing 7B model text generation at 53.13 tokens per second and 70B model text generation at 6.87 tokens per second.

Given its high-density memory allocation and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, this instance offers high resource density for memory-bound enterprise workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. This server is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive data processing, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs (90 physical cores) powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor and 1440.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 124350.65 and a Redis SET throughput of 210.86 million operations per second. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in CPU-based LLM inference, processing 7B model text generation at 53.13 tokens per second. This instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server with 180 vCPUs, 1440 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC 9B14 processors, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server with 180 vCPUs and 1440 GB RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and 1.4 TB of RAM for demanding enterprise database and memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors with 180 dedicated vCPUs and 90 physical cores
Equipped with 1440.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core performance, achieving 210.86 million Redis SET operations per second
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration instruction sets
Best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
